understanding.goal_understanding: Each level’s objective was to get all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked with a '+' at the same time. If there were multiple '+' tiles, every one had to be occupied simultaneously for the level to register WIN. Completing the final level would win the whole game run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Only one piece is controlled/selected at a time. MOVE_* actions made the currently selected piece slide in that direction until it hit an obstacle/boundary (not just a single-cell step), as seen when MOVE_UP moved from [12,21] to [12,18] and MOVE_RIGHT moved from [30,21] to [33,21]. CLICK attempted to reposition the selected piece toward the clicked coordinate; it succeeded when the destination was reachable (e.g., [30,21] -> [12,21]), and failed with 'did not change position' when blocked by walls/obstacles or when the click had no valid movement effect. Obstacles/walls prevent movement; in this run no successful pushing interactions were observed (clicks into blocked areas simply resulted in no movement).

orient_history: Movement keys appear to attempt 1-cell (3px) grid moves, but both pieces are enclosed. Hypothesis: CLICK may allow repositioning/teleporting a selected piece (or selecting a destination/goal) despite walls, since normal movement is impossible.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a selectable piece when clicking on its cell; MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) but is blocked by walls. Need to select the other piece and check if it has any available movement or interaction toward the goal.

CLICK appears to change which selectable is controlled (the 'player' position updates to the clicked selectable). Since movement is fully blocked by walls, progress likely requires using CLICK-based interactions (possibly selecting the other piece, or enabling a placement/teleport onto the goal).

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches the active/controlled piece (even if the log describes it as the selected piece 'moving'). Then, with the correct piece selected, another CLICK interaction (e.g., on the goal) may place it onto the goal despite arrow-movement being blocked.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move/teleport the selected piece to the clicked location if it is a valid reachable/allowed destination; clicking on the other selectable definitely swaps/relocates between the two selectable spots. Since MOVE_* is fully blocked, progress likely requires CLICKing into/onto the goal region to place a piece there (possibly ignoring normal movement).

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK is not the primary movement; it likely just changes which selectable is active (or attempts a teleport only between existing selectable positions). Actual navigation may require MOVE_* inputs, possibly moving a cursor/selector even when the piece itself is boxed, or interacting with the level state differently than CLICK.

CLICK seems to attempt to move the selected piece directly to the clicked coordinate when that destination is reachable/valid (earlier clicks moved between [9,21] and [18,21]); MOVE_* is standard grid-step movement but can get the piece stuck against walls.
CLICK likely changes which selectable is controlled (selects the clicked selectable), while MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece one cell (3 pixels) if not blocked.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a selectable piece at the clicked position (switching control). MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) if not blocked by walls/other pieces. Goal requires moving selectables onto goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to place/teleport the currently selected 3x3 piece to the clicked pixel position if that destination is not blocked by a wall; goals are non-collidable, so clicking on the goal should allow the piece to occupy it and trigger WIN (since there is only 1 goal).

CLICK likely changes which selectable piece is currently controlled (the engine reports this as the 'selected piece moved' because control transfers to the clicked piece’s position). If we can control the other piece, it may have a path toward the goal even though the current one is stuck.

New hypothesis: movement is on a 3-pixel (1-cell) grid and pieces collide with walls; CLICK teleports only to certain valid cells/regions, but I may be stuck in a sealed pocket with this piece. A non-CLICK action might either (a) confirm the pocket is immovable, or (b) trigger a selection/interaction change when movement is attempted while blocked.

CLICK likely selects a piece at the clicked position (and may also be used for special repositioning), while MOVE_* moves the currently selected 3x3 piece by 3 pixels if not colliding with walls/other pieces. Since the current selected piece is immobile, we must switch control to the other selectable to make progress toward the goal.

CLICK appears to reposition (or swap) selectables between valid open cells/occupied selectable cells, while MOVE attempts shift by 3 pixels but are often blocked due to 3x3 collision against walls. I suspect we need to rearrange the two selectables via CLICK to find a position with an opening toward the goal area.

CLICK on a selectable likely changes which piece is selected; CLICK on empty space may attempt to move the currently selected piece there if unobstructed. Since movement is currently blocked, switching selection is the next useful probe.

CLICK appears to either move the currently selected piece to a reachable empty tile (if connected/unblocked) or potentially change selection when clicking another selectable. Since movement is fully blocked, the only plausible progress is to change which piece is selected.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move the currently selected piece directly to the clicked pixel coordinate snapped on the 3px grid; the move fails if the destination cell is a wall/invalid. The earlier goal click likely failed due to misalignment (not multiple of 3) rather than distance/pathing.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK seems to *reposition* the selected piece but the current area is a tight pocket where standard MOVE may be needed to trigger a push/swap/unstick interaction with the adjacent selectable/walls. Since all MOVE directions are blocked, I will probe with a MOVE to see if any hidden mechanic (pushing the adjacent selectable, swapping, or sliding along walls) occurs despite the 'blocked' flag.

Clicks likely either (a) select a different selectable when clicking on it, or (b) attempt to move the currently-selected piece toward the clicked coordinate; MOVE_* shifts the selected piece by one cell when not blocked. Since the current selected piece is fully boxed in, progress requires switching selection to the other selectable and moving it onto the goal area.

CLICK seems to attempt to reposition the currently selected selectable directly to the clicked cell (if not colliding). Even if MOVE_RIGHT is blocked by wall-adjacency quirks, a direct CLICK onto an open goal cell may still place the piece onto the goal region.

Pieces move in 3-pixel (1-cell) increments with MOVE, but walls can fully trap a piece. CLICK seems to relocate the selected piece directly to the clicked top-left pixel if that placement is valid (not colliding with walls/other selectable). The goal object is at pixel [35,17] size 5x5, so to count as 'on goal' the selectable likely must be placed fully within that 5x5 region; a good aligned candidate is [36,18] (within x=35..39, y=17..21 and on the 3-pixel grid).

CLICK to empty/goal coordinates attempts to reposition the currently selected piece; movement is collision-based in pixel space (not semantic cells), so entering the goal likely requires clicking the goal’s exact pixel anchor/inside its rectangle rather than the semantic '+' approximation.

CLICK likely changes selection when clicking on another selectable, and otherwise attempts to move the currently selected piece if the destination is reachable/valid. Since the current selected piece is immobile, we should switch to the other selectable and try to move it toward the goal region.

New hypothesis (based only on observed outcomes): CLICK is primarily changing which selectable is active (it ‘jumps’ the selected focus between existing selectables), not moving through space. Actual movement is via MOVE_* but the currently selected piece is trapped, so I likely need to switch to the other selectable to reach the goal. Right now I must use a non-CLICK action due to the forced reframe constraint.

Movement (MOVE_*) is grid-step and respects walls. CLICK on a cell containing the other selectable swaps the two selectables’ positions (even through walls). CLICK to empty coordinates attempts to relocate but can fail if blocked by walls/line-of-sight. To make progress, we must swap to control the right-side piece near the goal.

CLICK appears to attempt to place the selected 3x3 piece with its top-left at the clicked pixel position, but placement fails if any part would overlap a wall. The goal is a small non-grid-aligned region (5x5), so clicking on grid-aligned goal cells may still collide; we likely need a precise top-left within the goal's 5x5 area (x 35-37, y 17-19) that avoids walls.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to place/move the currently selected 1x1 piece to the clicked pixel location if that location is not colliding with walls; goal tiles are non-collidable and should accept placement. We likely must get both selectables onto + tiles simultaneously; selection likely changes only when clicking directly on the other selectable.

CLICK may only succeed for certain valid tile-aligned destinations (or when interacting with another selectable), and my previous goal-click coordinates may not have been valid placements. Since MOVE is fully blocked for the current selected piece, the only remaining chance is to try a different tile-aligned CLICK position near/within the goal region that we have not tested yet.

CLICK likely teleports the selected piece’s top-left to the clicked pixel coordinate if the full piece footprint fits without overlapping walls. Since moves are blocked, winning likely requires a precise CLICK to align the 3x3 piece exactly over the 3x3 goal area (top-left at cell [11,5] => pixel [33,15]).

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK attempts to reposition/teleport the selected 3x3 piece to the clicked pixel location only if the full 3x3 footprint fits without overlapping any collidable wall tiles. The goal tile itself is non-collidable, but the surrounding wall geometry can still prevent entering the goal area. MOVE_* shifts by one cell (3 px) and is prevented if any part of the 3x3 would collide with a wall.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts shift by one 3px cell but is prevented by wall collision; CLICK attempts to relocate the selected 3x3 piece to the clicked pixel position if its full footprint is not colliding with walls. The current location is a dead-end, so only a successful CLICK into a non-walled part of the goal region can still produce WIN (likely by overlapping the goal area).

Actions appear to consume steps; with 0 steps remaining, further actions likely won’t execute or will have no effect. Movement is grid-aligned in 3-pixel (1 cell) increments; CLICK can relocate the selected piece only if the destination is reachable/unblocked (likely along a clear path). To win, both selectables must occupy goal tiles simultaneously, but we cannot reposition with no steps.

understanding.goal_understanding: Each level’s objective was to get all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked with a '+' at the same time. If there were multiple '+' tiles, every one had to be occupied simultaneously for the level to register WIN. Completing the final level would win the whole game run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Only one piece is controlled/selected at a time. MOVE_* actions made the currently selected piece slide in that direction until it hit an obstacle/boundary (not just a single-cell step), as seen when MOVE_UP moved from [12,21] to [12,18] and MOVE_RIGHT moved from [30,21] to [33,21]. CLICK attempted to reposition the selected piece toward the clicked coordinate; it succeeded when the destination was reachable (e.g., [30,21] -> [12,21]), and failed with 'did not change position' when blocked by walls/obstacles or when the click had no valid movement effect. Obstacles/walls prevent movement; in this run no successful pushing interactions were observed (clicks into blocked areas simply resulted in no movement).
